Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search email for the stipend duplicate-audit thread using search_emails (query: "duplicate receipt" OR "TechComfort" OR "stipend audit" OR "Dmitri" OR "Marcus" OR "BrightLoop" OR "STIP-2026-0094"). Open the full thread with get_thread (thread_id: from search/get_email_threads for the thread containing email_dmitri_receipt_audit_findings and email_marcus_qb_status_reply) to recover: three flagged rows (Kevin Tran / Vectral $89 paid 2026-03-08, Andre Washington / Mosaic $89 pending, Jordan Ekwueme / BrightLoop $45 pending), total exposure language ($223 / $134 on hold), Marcus QuickBooks reply confirming paid vs pending, and AM routing to client HR (do not use these emails to contact pilot clients per prompt).
OE 2: Search Slack #finance for Marcus's stipend-hold post and thread using channels_list (confirm C005 = finance) then conversations_search_messages (filter_in_channel: C005, search_query: "stipend" OR "hold" OR "duplicate" OR "Dmitri" OR "STIP-2026-0094" OR "Admin has placed holds"). Identify the parent message with ts 1775597280.000000 (two pending holds $134, Kevin Tran $89 already disbursed 3/8, BrightLoop Jordan $45 in cluster). Pull full thread with conversations_replies (channel_id: C005, thread_ts: 1775597280.000000) to capture nested replies: misleading "two pilot clients only" vs authoritative three-client cluster / global hash scope (e.g. moveops_emeka_diallo vs moveops_hana_kim when task inject rows from Tasks/9_Universe_inject.sql are present).
OE 3: Search Slack #engineering for duplicate-receipt and pilot context using conversations_search_messages (filter_in_channel: C003, search_query: "ENG-220" OR "duplicate" OR "BrightLoop" OR "hash" OR "receipt"). Read threaded context with conversations_replies (channel_id: C003, thread_ts: 1775598300.000000) for Layer 1/2, cross-client requirement, and filing of ENG-220. Capture any top-level bump that states ENG-220 is authoritative on three flagged submissions and BrightLoop must not be dropped (e.g. moveops_samira_tariq when task inject is present).
OE 4: List and open Expense Auto-Categorization / pilot-exit gating tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "pilot monitoring" OR "duplicate" OR "launch readiness" OR "pipeline hardening" OR "ENG-211" OR "ENG-217" OR "ENG-219" OR "ENG-220", project: Expense Auto-Categorization Engine as applicable). Use linear_get_issue (id: issue_pilot_monitoring / ENG-211, issue_pipeline_hardening / ENG-217, issue_launch_readiness_checklist / ENG-219, issue_stipend_duplicate_detection / ENG-220) for descriptions, states, assignees, and blocker language.
OE 5: List comments using linear_list_comments (issueId: issue_pilot_monitoring, issue_stipend_duplicate_detection, issue_pipeline_hardening, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, issue_audit_expenses). Recover: false-reject / false-approve incident notes and Elena CEO directive (auto-approve/deny paused) on pilot monitoring; Kevin Tran internet setup + first month edge (recStip000000017 / setup vs monthly cap) from comment_mina_two_incidents (issue_pilot_monitoring) and comment_lena_deploy_verified (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed); Dmitri's duplicate forensics from comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery (on issue_audit_expenses / ENG-216) and comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context (on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection / ENG-220), including Airtable record IDs rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand.
OE 6: Retrieve the three stipend Airtable rows tied to the duplicate cluster using airtable_list_bases / airtable_list_tables to locate table name "Stipend Transactions", then airtable_get_record (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Stipend Transactions, record_id: rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) and/or airtable_search_records (field_name/value e.g. Employee Name + "Kevin Tran" or Review Notes containing "TechComfort"). Confirm: Kevin Tran ergonomic keyboard $89.00 Approved with audit duplicate-hash notes; Andre Washington $89.00 Pending Approval / duplicate hash; Jordan Ekwueme monitor stand $45.00 Pending Approval / template match, aligned with #finance Marcus post (only cite amounts present in tools).
OE 7: Optional cross-check stipend bills in QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria to match DocNumber STIP-2026-0089, STIP-2026-0094, STIP-2026-0097 or descriptions mentioning TechComfort / admin hold) and optionally quickbooks_get_bill (id: bill_stipend_tran_keyboard, bill_stipend_washington_keyboard_hold, bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor_hold) to align paid vs pending / hold language with bill Line descriptions; do not invent totals beyond messages/records.
OE 8: Read Lena's INBOX for engineering follow-up using list_emails (folder_name: INBOX) and/or search_emails (query: "ENG-220" OR "BrightLoop" OR "hash backfill" OR "ENG-217"). If task inject is loaded, open get_email_by_id (email_id: email_dmitri_dup_audit_followup_lena_task5) for global hash index and backfill sequencing after ENG-217.
OE 9: Update pilot monitoring using linear_update_issue (id: issue_pilot_monitoring): reconciled duplicate scope (three clients / three flagged stipend rows), hold vs paid facts consistent with #finance Marcus post + Airtable + Dmitri/Marcus email thread, auto-decisions remain off per Elena, which source to mirror in engineering tickets (#finance + stipend records + audit email over ad-hoc Slack takes), and remaining eng blockers without promising re-enablement dates to AMs.
OE 10: Update duplicate-receipt workstream using linear_update_issue (id: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) and/or linear_create_comment (issueId: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection): Layer 1 global hash + Layer 2 metadata, cross-client requirement, exposure figures only as already stated in comms/records ($89 paid, $134 pending, $223 cluster if quoted verbatim from existing text), and explicit sequencing that receipt hash backfill runs after ENG-217 pipeline type-enforcement is in production.
OE 11: If Kevin Tran internet setup fee edge (Airtable recStip000000017) is not already linked from pilot/duplicate discussion, add linear_create_comment (issueId: issue_pilot_monitoring or issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) referencing that row and the setup-vs-monthly-cap parser edge from existing Linear comments.
OE 12: Look up emails for send and calendar using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Dmitri Volkov" then "Samira Tariq" then "Lena Björkström" or equivalent single-query search). Confirm dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com.
OE 13: Post a short factual summary to #engineering using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C003, user_id: moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, payload: markdown mentioning @Samira / Samira Tariq): reconciled duplicate scope (three clients), paid vs on-hold facts from records, no new dollar math beyond tools, no "almost turning auto back on" or dates for AMs, no client outreach.
OE 14: Email Dmitri with concrete technical asks using send_email (sender: lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, recipients: ["dmitri.volkov@moveops.com"], subject/body): hash backfill plan, dependency on ENG-217 deployment, global index scope, specific questions, no vague "let's sync" only; align with stipend audit email thread and inject follow-up if present.
OE 15: Resolve "next Tuesday morning" (from eval anchor April 26, 2026 → Tuesday April 28, 2026) and mutual availability using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to and/or calendar_search_events for Lena and Samira for the 2026-04-28 morning window; if calendars are empty, pick a concrete 30-minute slot in that window absent conflicts.
OE 16: Book the working session using calendar_add_calendar_event (title, start_datetime, end_datetime spanning 30 minutes on 2026-04-28 morning, attendees: ["lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com", "samira.tariq@moveops.com"], description tying duplicate detector + ENG-217 sequencing / pilot exit alignment).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A linear_update_issue call updated the issue with id issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211) and/or a linear_create_comment call used issueId issue_pilot_monitoring.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1A linear_create_comment call was made with issueId 'issue_pilot_monitoring' at timestamp 2026-03-31T02:21:13.915070Z, adding a detailed reconciliation comment to ENG-211.
✓Run #2The trajectory includes a linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_pilot_monitoring' that added a comprehensive reconciled status comment to ENG-211.
✓Run #3The trajectory contains a linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_pilot_monitoring', confirmed in tool_call_result_24.json showing the comment was successfully created on that issue.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows a linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_pilot_monitoring' at timestamp 2026-03-31T02:22:02.760377Z. The comment body is substantial and covers the reconciled facts.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_pilot_monitoring' at timestamp 2026-03-31T02:22:40.380057Z, adding a detailed comment to ENG-211.
✓Run #6A linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_pilot_monitoring' is present in the trajectory at 2026-03-31T02:22:13.164044Z.
2 The linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment affecting issue_pilot_monitoring (description, title, or body) states that auto-approve and auto-deny for expenses remain off, paused, or disabled per Elena Rostova (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The linear_create_comment body for issue_pilot_monitoring explicitly states: 'Auto-approve/deny remains OFF per Elena's 3/17 directive. No re-enablement date has been set; eng + finance must align first.'
✓Run #2The linear_create_comment body for issue_pilot_monitoring explicitly states: 'Auto-approve/deny: Still disabled per Elena's 3/17 directive. No re-enablement date set.'
✓Run #3The linear_create_comment on issue_pilot_monitoring explicitly states: 'Auto-approve/auto-deny remains **OFF** per Elena's directive' in the Engineering Blockers section.
✓Run #4The linear_create_comment for issue_pilot_monitoring explicitly states: 'Remains **OFF** per Elena's directive. No re-enablement date committed.' This satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #5The linear_create_comment body for issue_pilot_monitoring explicitly states: 'Auto-approve / auto-deny: **OFF** per Elena's directive (2026-03-15). All submissions >$100 require manual verification.'
✓Run #6The ENG-211 comment body explicitly states: '**Auto-approve/deny remains OFF** per Elena's directive (confirmed deployed 3/18). No date for re-enablement has been set; eng + finance must align first.'
3 The final response states which sources engineering tickets should mirror when Slack or Linear disagree with stipend records or finance posts - specifically Marcus's stipend-hold narrative in #finance, the Airtable stipend rows, and the Marcus–Dmitri email audit thread as preferred over inconsistent ad-hoc Slack-only summaries (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly states: 'For duplicate scope, hold/paid status, and dollar amounts: ENG-220 + Airtable records are the primary sources. Marcus's #finance post and Hana's thread replies are consistent. Emeka's narrower framing should not be propagated into duplicate-detection work.' This mirrors the preference for ENG-220, Airtable, and Marcus's #finance over ad-hoc Slack framing.
✓Run #2The final response includes an explicit 'Source of truth hierarchy' section listing: 1) Airtable (approval status/amounts), 2) Marcus's #finance posts + Hana's tracker (hold IDs/financial exposure), 3) ENG-220 (technical scope), 4) Slack threads (context only, sometimes incomplete).
✓Run #3The final response identifies ENG-220 as authoritative (backed by Airtable records, Marcus's #finance posts, and Hana's reconciliation tracker) and explicitly calls out Emeka's ad-hoc Slack reading as wrong versus Hana's correction in the official #finance thread. The ENG-211 comment also states 'Authoritative source for duplicate scope: ENG-220. Mirror that ticket's three-row table for any status updates' and 'Airtable records match finance posts exactly.'
✓Run #4The final response states 'These numbers are confirmed across Airtable, Marcus's #finance post, Hana's thread reply, and ENG-220' and designates ENG-220 as 'the authoritative source.' It also quotes Samira's directive to treat ENG-220 as authoritative on scope. This identifies the preferred sources (Airtable, #finance, ENG-220) over ad-hoc Slack summaries.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly recommends '**Airtable** is authoritative for hold vs. paid status' and '**ENG-220** is the authoritative ticket for duplicate scope/facts', calling out Emeka's incorrect Slack post (pilot-only scoping) as wrong and Hana/Samira's corrections as right. Airtable stipend rows are identified as the source to mirror.
✗Run #6The final response does not explicitly tell the user which source to mirror when sources disagree. It notes all sources agree and ENG-220 was correct, but does not provide explicit source-of-truth ranking guidance (e.g., prefer Airtable + Marcus's #finance post over ad-hoc Slack) directed at Lena for engineering ticket writing.

36 The linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment affecting issue_stipend_duplicate_detection includes body or description text that references cross-client scope, three flagged stipend submissions, or a global hash index requirement (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The linear_create_comment body for issue_stipend_duplicate_detection states 'Three clients (Vectral, Mosaic, BrightLoop)', 'BrightLoop is not a pilot client but IS in the duplicate cluster', 'The DuplicateDetector (Layers 1-3) must be cross-client/global as spec'd', and references a global receipt hash index/backfill.
✓Run #2The linear_create_comment body for issue_stipend_duplicate_detection explicitly mentions 'cross-client scope', three records confirmed (Vectral, Mosaic, BrightLoop), and states 'The DuplicateDetector hash index must be global per the cross-client requirement in Layer 3.'
✓Run #3The linear_create_comment on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection states 'The hash index and DuplicateDetector must be **global/cross-client** per the ticket spec' and 'BrightLoop is in the cluster', explicitly referencing cross-client scope and global hash index requirement.
✓Run #4The linear_create_comment for issue_stipend_duplicate_detection states 'Scope confirmation: Three clients (Vectral, Mosaic, BrightLoop)' and 'The hash index and metadata fingerprint storage **must be global**' — explicitly covering cross-client scope and global hash index requirements.
✓Run #5The linear_create_comment body for issue_stipend_duplicate_detection includes a 3-client table and explicitly states: 'DuplicateDetector hash index must be **global** (cross-client), not scoped to pilot clients. The three flagged rows span 3 different client companies.'
✓Run #6The ENG-220 comment references 'cross-client scope', lists all three flagged stipend submissions in a table, and explicitly states 'global receipt hash backfill' and the need for a global vs per-client hash index.
